meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no understanding of APIs (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve No API Knowledge
content.seo_description: Master user onboarding without deep API expertise. A premium case study for freelancers leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop wrestling with complex API documentation. By using Antigravity to abstract endpoint logic and Claude Code to automate the implementation, you can build professional, high-conversion user onboarding flows in hours, not weeks. This approach turns technical debt into a repeatable, high-margin asset for your freelance business.
content.intro:
The client is breathing down your neck, the deadline is looming, and your project requirements demand a seamless user onboarding flow that communicates with external APIs you barely understand. You're stuck in a cycle of reading dry documentation, debugging status codes, and burning billable hours on tasks that should be trivial.

But what if you didn't have to be an API expert to build like one? By shifting your workflow to use Antigravity for rapid abstraction and Claude Code for intelligent, context-aware implementation, you can bypass the 'API wall' entirely. This is how top-tier freelancers ship complex SaaS features while maintaining a 4x velocity advantage over the competition.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding Schema
steps.description: Map out the data points required for your user onboarding. Focus on the 'happy path' to minimize API calls and friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a SaaS user onboarding flow including name, company, and role, mapping these to a standard REST API format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a TypeScript interface for the onboarding schema defined in antigravity.json'

steps.title: Abstract API Logic with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean wrapper around your backend service, hiding the complexity of headers, authentication, and error handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable API client wrapper in TypeScript for user creation, including built-in error handling and status code logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file ./src/api/client.ts --instruction 'Implement the generated client with Axios'

steps.title: Build the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Create a robust state machine to handle the user journey, ensuring the UI remains responsive even when API calls are pending.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a state machine logic for a 3-step onboarding process: Idle, Loading, Success, Error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a React hook useOnboardingState that manages the onboarding process flow'

steps.title: Integrate UI Components
steps.description: Connect your state management to your frontend components, ensuring smooth transitions and immediate user feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component for the first step of onboarding that utilizes the useOnboardingState hook.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file ./src/components/OnboardingForm.tsx --instruction 'Integrate the hook and provide loading states'

steps.title: Automate Testing and Deployment
steps.description: Verify your implementation with automated tests to ensure API reliability before shipping to your client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for the onboarding API client focusing on success, 401 error, and 500 server error scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Run the test suite and fix any integration mismatches detected'

pro_tips: Always use a 'local-first' strategy during development to avoid hitting API rate limits while debugging UI flows.
Centralize your API error logging in a single utility file; it saves hours of troubleshooting during client UAT sessions.
Use mock servers (via Antigravity) to simulate edge cases like slow network latency to test your UI's loading states.
Treat your onboarding schema as a living document; keep it in the root directory to allow Claude Code to reference it in every session.
Implement 'optimistic UI updates' to make your app feel instantaneous, even if the backend API is slow.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, implement a basic analytics event tracker to monitor drop-off rates, then scale your API client to handle secondary features like user profile management.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the technical details and start shipping products that scale. By mastering these tools, you're not just building features; you're building a scalable freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
